currentUse: Unknown — the church was sold after the 2008 closure (confirmed as "closed and sold" by Draugas, May 2026), but no source found identifies the buyer or the building's present occupant or use. Last visual confirmation of the building standing is an August 2015 photograph; no demolition reports were found.
summary: Our Lady of Šiluva (Lithuanian, est. 1907, formerly St. Louis; the current building dates to 1967 after fires destroyed earlier structures) stands on Main St. in the village of Maizeville, Gilberton borough, Schuylkill County. Under the Diocese of Allentown's restructuring plan announced May 31, 2008, it was one of seven parishes merged into St. Joseph in Mahanoy City, renamed Blessed Teresa of Calcutta effective July 15, 2008, with Rev. Kevin Gallagher as pastor. Šiluva parishioners were among four parish groups who filed appeals asking the bishop to reconsider the closings. The building was photographed still standing in August 2015, and Global True Lithuania records that its Lithuanian-detail interior and stained-glass windows were slated for removal after closure. A May 2026 Draugas article confirms the church was subsequently sold and that its large Vytautas Kazimieras Jonynas stained-glass window was rescued and now adorns the stage of the Lithuanian Youth Center (Jaunimo centras) in Chicago. The successor parish, St. Teresa of Calcutta in Mahanoy City, remains active. No source found identifies the building's buyer, sale price, or present-day use.

developments.headline: Merged into new Mahanoy City parish under Allentown restructuring
developments.detail: Under the Diocese of Allentown plan announced May 31, 2008 (which closed 32 churches diocese-wide), Our Lady of Siluva, Maizeville, together with St. Canicus, Assumption BVM, Sacred Heart, St. Casimir and St. Fidelis, merged into St. Joseph, Mahanoy City; the combined parish was renamed Blessed Teresa of Calcutta effective July 15, 2008, with Rev. Kevin Gallagher as pastor.

sources.title: Some Schuylkill County Churches Learn Their Fates

developments.headline: Parishioners appealed the closing to the bishop
developments.detail: As the seven-parish merger into Blessed Teresa of Calcutta was celebrated at the former St. Joseph Church, parishioners of Our Lady of Siluva were among four parish groups who filed appeals asking the Bishop of Allentown to reconsider the closings.

sources.title: 7 Churches Become One in Allentown Diocese

developments.headline: Building photographed still standing
developments.detail: A Wikimedia Commons photograph dated August 15, 2015 shows the front of Our Lady of Siluva RC Church (Lithuanian, est. 1907, formerly St. Louis) on Main St., Maizeville, Gilberton borough — the most recent visual confirmation found of the building standing.

sources.title: File:Our Lady of Siluva RC Church, Maizeville PA 01.JPG

developments.headline: Rescued Jonynas stained-glass window installed at Chicago Lithuanian Youth Center
developments.detail: Draugas reported that the large Vytautas Kazimieras Jonynas stained-glass window, rescued from the closed and sold Šiluvos Marijos church in Maizeville, PA, now adorns the stage of the Jaunimo centras (Lithuanian Youth Center) in Chicago. The article confirms the building was sold but does not name the rescuer, the buyer, or the rescue date.

Shenandoah and southern Coal Region: Lithuanian heritage
Church of St. Teresa of Calcutta, Mahanoy City, Pennsylvania, USA

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. Refinements: (1) the 2008 "closure" was formally a merger — Šiluva was one of six churches merged into St. Joseph, Mahanoy City, renamed Blessed (now Saint) Teresa of Calcutta effective July 15, 2008, and parishioners filed an appeal with the bishop; (2) the archive's "building sold" is corroborated by Draugas (May 2026), which describes the church as "closed and sold"; (3) the archive credits the "Šaknys project" with the Jonynas stained-glass rescue — the rescue and the window's relocation to the Chicago Jaunimo centras stage are verified, but no opened source names Šaknys as the rescuer, so that attribution stands unverified rather than contradicted. Founding year refinement: sources give the Lithuanian parish (as St. Louis) as established 1907, with the current building from 1967 and renamed Our Lady of Šiluva after fires.
gaps: Searched (English and Lithuanian): parish name + Maizeville/Gilberton with sold/demolished/for sale/auction/buyer terms, Schuylkill County property angles, Jonynas + Šaknys + vitražai, appeal/Vatican outcome, and successor-parish pages. Not found: (1) buyer identity, sale year, and sale price of the church property; (2) the building's current occupant, use, or condition — last visual confirmation standing is the August 2015 Wikimedia photo, 11 years stale, so 'standing' rests on that photo plus absence of any demolition report; (3) outcome of the 2008 parishioners' appeal; (4) verification of the 'Šaknys project' attribution for the stained-glass rescue and the rescue date; (5) any reunion, preservation, or community activity specific to Maizeville. Note: a Ukrainian Catholic parish (1408 Main St, Maizeville) also exists in the village — care was taken not to conflate it; all findings above are confirmed to concern the Lithuanian parish (est. 1907, formerly St. Louis).
